subject: Databases
title: Materialized Path
description: A short lesson to help you apply Materialized Path.

hook.text: Each row stores its full path: /electronics/phones/smartphones. Finding all children of electronics is a LIKE query.

buildup.text: WHERE path LIKE '/electronics/%' returns all items under electronics. No recursion needed.

discovery.text: The path encodes the full ancestry. Depth is the number of separators. Sorting by path gives tree order.

twist.text: Moving a subtree means updating every descendant's path. One move can touch thousands of rows.

climax.text: Use materialized paths for read-heavy hierarchies where moves are rare: category trees, URL structures.

punchline.text: Materialized path: fast reads without recursion. Expensive moves.

quiz.question: What is the downside of materialized path for hierarchies?
options: Moving a subtree requires updating the path of every descendant row
It cannot represent hierarchies deeper than 3 levels
Materialized paths use too much memory
